headline: Assessing Visa (V) Valuation After Recent Share Price Pullback
summary: Why Visa (V) is drawing fresh attention Visa (V) is back on investor watchlists after a recent pullback, with the stock down about 13% year to date and roughly 15% over the past 3 months. See our latest analysis for Visa. The recent 30 day share price return of a 4.67% decline and 90 day share price return of a 14.99% decline have softened Visa’s momentum, even though the 3 year total shareholder return of 35.48% and 5 year total shareholder return of 42.02% remain positive. headline: Stablecoins to transact $1.5 quadrillion? Yes, Chainalysis really just made that forecast
summary: Stablecoin volumes will hit $1.5 quadrillion by 2035, said Chainaysis. Projection hinges on $100 trillion wealth transfer to crypto-native generation. Transaction counts could match Visa and Mastercard by the mid 2030s.

headline: Visa Opens the Door to AI-Driven Shopping for Businesses Worldwide
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 08, 2026--Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) today unveiled Intelligent Commerce Connect, a new solution that makes it easier for businesses to connect to and participate in AI-powered commerce. Intelligent Commerce Connect acts as a network, protocol, and token vault-agnostic ‘on ramp’ to agentic commerce for agent builders, merchants, and enablers.
